name: Perplexica
tagline: Privacy-focused AI answering engine that runs on your own hardware with cited sources
metaDescription: Perplexica, now Vane, is an open-source privacy-focused AI answering engine. Search the web with cited sources using local Ollama models or cloud LLMs, self-hosted.
overview: Perplexica is an open-source AI answering engine that runs entirely on your own hardware. The project is now developed under the name Vane. It searches the internet and returns clear answers with cited sources, so you can check where every claim comes from.

It works with local large language models through Ollama as well as cloud providers such as OpenAI, Anthropic Claude, Google Gemini, and Groq. Web search is handled by a bundled SearxNG instance, which queries many search engines at once while keeping your identity private.
features: Works with all major AI providers: local models via Ollama, plus OpenAI, Anthropic Claude, Google Gemini, and Groq, so you can mix and match models.
Smart search modes: Speed Mode for quick answers, Balanced Mode for everyday use, and Quality Mode for deep research.
Choose your sources: search the general web, discussions, or academic papers, and limit a search to specific domains when you know where to look.
Private web search powered by SearxNG, with image and video search alongside text results.
File uploads: add PDFs, text files, or images and ask questions about them.
Helpful widgets (weather, calculations, stock prices), smart suggestions as you type, a Discover feed, and locally saved search history.

gettingStarted.intro: Vane can be installed with Docker (recommended) or without it. The Docker image bundles both Vane and SearxNG, so no extra setup is needed. After it starts, open http://localhost:3000 and configure your API keys and models in the setup screen.

steps.heading: Run with Docker (recommended)
steps.body: Pull and start the Vane container, which includes the bundled SearxNG search engine. The volume keeps your data and uploaded files between restarts.

steps.heading: Or build from source with Docker
steps.body: Clone the repository, then build and run the image yourself if you need more control.
steps.code:
git clone https://github.com/ItzCrazyKns/Vane.git
docker build -t vane .
docker run -d -p 3000:3000 -v vane-data:/home/vane/data --name vane vane

steps.heading: Or install without Docker
steps.body: First install SearXNG and enable both JSON format and the Wolfram Alpha search engine in its settings. Then clone, install dependencies, build, and start Vane.
steps.code:
git clone https://github.com/ItzCrazyKns/Vane.git
cd Vane
npm i
npm run build
npm run start

steps.heading: Open and configure
steps.body: Navigate to http://localhost:3000 to finish setup and enter your settings (API keys, models, and the SearxNG URL) in the setup screen.

useCases: Run a private, self-hosted alternative to web AI search engines, keeping your queries on your own machine.
Get answers with cited sources by combining local Ollama models with live web search through SearxNG.
Do deep research across the web, discussions, or academic papers using Quality Mode and domain-limited searches.
Upload documents like PDFs and ask questions about their contents.
